stimulus: While reviewing seafloor sediment cores in 2018, marine geologist Yuki Tanaka identified an unusual layer of microscopic glass beads dating to roughly 12,800 years ago, suggesting that a fragmenting cosmic body had showered the region with debris. ______blank the rare deposit was named the "Tanaka horizon" in recognition of the geologist who first characterized it.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. "Fittingly" logically signals that naming a sediment layer after Yuki Tanaka is an appropriate outcome, since Tanaka is the geologist who first identified and characterized that deposit.

Choice A is incorrect because "however" illogically signals a contrast between the identification of the deposit and its naming, when the naming is in fact an apt outcome of the identification. Choice B is incorrect because "in other words" illogically signals that the naming sentence merely restates the previous information about Tanaka's identification of the deposit; instead, it describes a separate, fitting consequence. Choice D is incorrect because "similarly" illogically signals that the naming is a parallel case to the identification, when it is in fact an apt outcome of it.
